mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with improving labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months produce negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 2.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays near 2%, indicating real rates remain accommodative. This monetary accommodation compresses equity risk premiums and supports forward equity valuations, producing positive 20-day returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with contained inflation
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or the interaction coefficient between real rate proxy and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900,000 annualized units signal sustained residential construction demand and household formation, indicating broad-based economic expansion that supports equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sustained HOUST > 900k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.3% while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.2%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration that reduces earnings growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of periods meeting both trigger conditions, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise does not produce significantly lower returns than either signal alone (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates to below 2% annualized while real GDP (GDPC1) growth remains below 2.5%, indicating consumer demand weakness that constrains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: demand-constrained expansion with slowing consumer activity
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 48% of periods with RSAFS growth < 2%, or Rank IC between RSAFS deceleration and 5-day returns is below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 5.0% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150,000 persons monthly, signaling tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate cost burdens, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods meeting both conditions, or the interaction effect between tight labor conditions and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rises above 2.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling monetary tightening in response to inflation that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC between the tightening signal and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates with month-over-month growth above 0.4% while unemployment (UNRATE) remains stable below 5.5%, signaling robust supply-side expansion and efficient labor utilization that supports earnings growth and positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.4% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of periods with sustained INDPRO growth > 0.4%, or Rank IC between INDPRO acceleration and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
